## 🤝 Cross-Team Insights

### 1. Healthcare Cluster = Full Coverage, Zero Sequence
Scout added 3 healthcare leads today (ER at Boulder's Edge, UMC East Charleston, Village at St Rose) — 10+ weeks of consistent healthcare finds. Still no healthcare Instantly sequence. These leads land in "New" bucket with no activation path. **This is Week 10+ of a known gap. Relay must build the healthcare sequence this week.**

### 2. DHL + DMV NLV = North Las Vegas Employer Cluster
Scout surfaced DHL (500+ employees, 24/7, Donovan Way) and Nevada DMV Commercial (660 reviews, CDL office) — both in North Las Vegas. This is a new geographic cluster forming. Relay's existing NLV sequences may not be tuned for 24/7 shift workers. The break room pitch angle applies directly.

### 3. Southern Pines = Affordable Housing Pitch Track (Captive Audience)
Southern Pines (Nevada HAND, 50-80% AMI, grand opened March 2026) maps to the "captive necessity" pitch established for Patriot Housing in Feb water-coolers: "Residents with limited transportation depend on on-site resources." This is NOT the residential amenity pitch. Distinct framing required.

### 4. Ralph's Playbook = Real Sales Intelligence Now Available
Ralph committed 300+ community posts + 40+ transcripts from Mighty Networks. The `playbook.md` update contains actionable machine-specific intel: Dr. Pepper and Celsius as top sellers, $1,500 Vistar threshold for discounts, SandStar cooler specs for garden-style apartments. **Relay should pull from playbook.md when building proposals and coaching cards.**

### 5. West Henderson Fieldhouse = Pre-RFP Window (Get There Now)
KemperSports operates. Fall 2026 opening. Pre-opening vendor conversations typically happen 90-120 days out — we're in that window NOW. Scout has the contact angle (city of Henderson + KemperSports HQ). This is the easiest time to get a meeting; it gets harder after groundbreaking dust settles.

### 6. Carnegie Heights = One Signature Away From First Revenue
The only Negotiating prospect. Makenna has already shown intent — she's "reviewing logistics." The approver chain question was flagged in Feb water-cooler and again in March. Answer it now: "Happy to prep paperwork — who else should I include?" One question, one call, potentially first MRR.

## 💡 New Learnings (to append)

### Affordable Housing Captive Necessity Pitch (Apr 2)
Southern Pines (Nevada HAND, 240 units, 50-80% AMI, March 2026) is the second affordable housing lead (after Patriot Housing Feb). Residents have limited transportation — vending is a necessity, not an amenity. Never use amenity framing on affordable/public housing. Lead with: "Residents who can't easily leave depend on on-site resources for daily needs." This is the same captive-audience psychology as dialysis, not the residential amenity pitch.

### Mighty Networks Playbook = Machine-Level Sales Intel Available
Ralph's Apr 2 commit includes Dr. Pepper + Celsius as confirmed top sellers, Bold Spicy Carbonara ramen as an unexpected high performer, $1,500 Vistar order threshold for 2% discount, and SandStar cooler as the right fit for garden-style apartment common rooms. Relay should reference `research/playbook.md` when building proposals — this is field-validated intel from 300+ operator conversations, not guesswork.

### Pre-RFP Relationship = Strongest Entry Point (Fall 2026 Opens)
West Henderson Fieldhouse (KemperSports, fall 2026) is the template case: approach the operator 4-6 months before opening, before the RFP process formalizes. Once RFP is out, you're in a competitive bid. Before it, you're a preferred conversation. Use for: West Henderson Fieldhouse, Four Seasons Private Residences, any fall 2026 opening.
